manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: snowsuit, macaroni, mustard, motorcycle, bourbon, chimp, doorknob, chalkboard, ladder.
Your opponent's team words are: pantyhose, elbow, basket, bedsheet, taxicab, gravy, hamburger, granite.
Distractor words are: chipmunk, rainfall, sideburns, umbrella, flower, bluebird, camcorder.
The kill words are: spear.

message.reasoning:
We need to provide a single-word clue and list of which of our team's words it relates to. Must be semantically related but not be any of the words. The clue should be single word.

Our team words: snowsuit, macaroni, mustard, motorcycle, bourbon, chimp, doorknob, chalkboard, ladder.

We need to avoid opponent words, distractors, kill. We can choose any number.

Goal to maximize but also ensure clear. We can maybe clue "school" for chalkboard, ladder (maybe school ladder? hmm). "Construction" for ladder, doorknob? Not great.

Maybe "pasta" for macaroni only.

Better to clue "motor" for motorcycle, motor? Not sure.

Maybe "barrel" for bourbon (aged in barrel), also maybe "snowsuit" (Barrel? no). "chimp" maybe "ape". clue "ape" is not allowed because it's similar to chimp (target word) but clue cannot be same as any word in lists. "ape" not in list, fine. "ape" relates to chimp only, maybe also to monkey? But fine, only target word chimp. That's minimal.

We could combine multiple, maybe "construction" for ladder, chalkboard (classroom?), doorknob (buildings). Hmm.

Maybe "road" for motorcycle, ladder? No.

Perhaps "spice" for mustard, macaroni (food), maybe also bourbon? Not spice.

Could clue "food" for macaroni, mustard, bourbon (drink), maybe also chimp? No.

Maybe "winter" for snowsuit, ladder (maybe not). Hmm.

We want something that connects multiple target words but not others to avoid confusion. Likely "classroom" for chalkboard, ladder (some classrooms have ladders?), doorknob (classroom door). Could be ambiguous.

Maybe "motorcycle" and "bourbon" are both associated with "America"? Not.

"Barrel" connects bourbon (aged), mustard? Not. "Chimp" and "motorcycle" no.

Better to give separate clues each turn. The instruction: "Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words..." So just one clue now.

Thus we could choose clue "food" -> relates to macaroni, mustard, bourbon (drink), maybe also chimp (eats food). But risk opponent guess "hamburger" (opponent) as it's also food. That might be dangerous: if partner guesses opponent's word, turn ends. So maybe avoid "food".

Maybe clue "school" => chalkboard, ladder (used in school for books?), doorknob (school doors). Could be okay, but opponent words "basket", "bedsheet", "taxicab", etc. Not likely. But cling.

Clue "classroom" likely leads to chalkboard primarily. Ladder maybe not obvious.

Better choose clue "writing" => chalkboard, doorknob? Not.

Maybe clue "climbing" => ladder, maybe also motorcycle (riding)? Not.

Clue "cold" => snowsuit, maybe also bourbon (served cold), but not strong.

Could choose "French" => mustard (French mustard), macaroni (pasta Italian), not.

Maybe we should limit to one word to avoid risk. Choose "chimpanzee" but clue cannot be part of target words; "chimpanzee" includes chimp? It includes "chimp", which is a target word, but clue cannot contain parts of them? The spec: "clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them." So "chimpanzee" contains "chimp", which is a target word, so not allowed.

Maybe "primates" includes "ape"? Not target. That's okay. "Primates" relates to chimp only. So we can do:

CLUE: primates
TARGETS: chimp

That's safe.

But maybe we can do multiple: "motor" for motorcycle, "motor" also for ladder? No.

"Vintage" for bourbon, also maybe snowsuit? Not.

Probably best to give a single clear clue for one word to avoid mistakes. So output:

CLUE: primates
TARGETS: chimp

Make sure format exactly.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide a comma-separated list of up to 1 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'primates'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer.

pantyhose, flower, taxicab, gravy, camcorder, elbow, chalkboard, sideburns, snowsuit, chimp, basket, granite, spear, bourbon, motorcycle, umbrella, ladder, bluebird, hamburger, bedsheet, macaroni, rainfall, mustard, chipmunk, doorknob

message.reasoning:
The user asks: "Provide a comma-separated list of up to 1 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'primates'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer."

We need choose up to 1 word that is most closely associated with primates. The list includes "chimp" which is a primate. So answer: "GUESS: chimp". Must be comma-separated list of up to 1 words, so just one word is fine. No extra
